scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from date fixed for performance, or if no date fixed, when plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff entered into an Agreement to Sell with the Defendant for the purchase of residential Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. The said Agreement to Sell is reduced to writing and signed by both parties, a copy of which is annexed hereto as **Annexure A**.

6. On the date of the execution of the Agreement to Sell, i.e., 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant via RTGS. The transaction bears UTR No. HDFC20240110987654. The receipt of the said payment is annexed hereto as **Annexure B**.

7. As per the terms of the Agreement to Sell, the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution and registration of the final Sale Deed. The Plaintiff has been keeping the balance amount ready and available for payment since the due date.

8. The Plaintiff has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the contractual obligations under the Agreement to Sell from the date of its execution until the date of filing this plaint.

BREACH OF CONTRACT

9. On 05.06.2024, prior to the deadline for the balance payment, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff expressly refusing to execute the Sale Deed. The Defendant cited the receipt of a higher offer from a third party as the reason for his refusal. A copy of the Defendant's refusal letter is annexed hereto as **Annexure C**.

10. To compel the Defendant to perform his contractual obligation, the Plaintiff issued a Legal Notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the Sale Deed as per the Agreement. The Legal Notice along with the postal receipt is annexed hereto as **Annexure D**.

11. Despite receipt of the Legal Notice, the Defendant has refused to execute the Sale Deed or refund the advance amount. The Defendant has failed and neglected to perform his part of the contract, thereby committing a breach of the Agreement to Sell.

DAMAGES

12. Due to the wrongful refusal of the Defendant to execute the Sale Deed, the Plaintiff's advance payment of Rs. 25,00,000/- remains locked with the Defendant without any return on investment, causing financial detriment to the Plaintiff.

13. The Plaintiff has been compelled to incur expenses towards stamp duty, court fees, and legal costs in filing the present suit solely due to the Defendant's breach of contract. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover these costs and any compensation arising from the delay in performance.

LEGAL BASIS

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Sections 10 and 14 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

15. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform his/her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

16. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

17. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

18. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 20.06.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

19. The cause of action is a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event initially, but the Plaintiff's right to performance subsists until the contract is fulfilled or finally denied.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

21. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree of specific performance directing the Defendant to perform and complete the agreement dated 10.01.2024 in favour of the Plaintiff;
    (b) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the necessary conveyance / sale deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration;
    (c) In the alternative, award compensation / damages in lieu of specific performance;
    (d) Award costs of the suit;
    (e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

Annexure D — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 21 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act, 1956,
Having its registered office at: MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon, aged 40 years.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A limited liability partnership registered under the LLP Act, 2008,
Having its office at: Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh, aged 48 years.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

2. The suit is valued above the specified value of {{SPECIFIED_VALUE}} as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

3. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

4. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

5. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

6. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from when contract is broken; or for continuing breach, when it ceases. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

7. The Plaintiff has complied with Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. {{MEDIATION_COMPLIANCE_DETAILS}}

FACTS OF THE CASE

8. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a Software Development Agreement for the development of an ERP system, with a total contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-. A copy of the said Agreement is annexed hereto as **Annexure A**.

9. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid the first milestone payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant on 01.02.2024 via NEFT (UTR: ICIC20240201456789). A copy of the NEFT receipt evidencing said payment is annexed hereto as **Annexure B**.

10. As per the terms stipulated in {{AGREEMENT_CLAUSE_NUMBER}} of the Agreement, the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system by 01.05.2024.

BREACH OF CONTRACT

11. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module or any part of the software by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024.

12. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant communicated its decision to abandon the project entirely without completing the deliverables. Copies of the email correspondence evidencing such abandonment are annexed hereto as **Annexure C**.

13. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 demanding performance and refund of the advance amount. A copy of the legal notice is annexed hereto as **Annexure E**.

14. The Defendant has failed to provide any satisfactory response to the legal notice as per {{LEGAL_NOTICE_RESPONSE_DETAILS}} and has not refunded the advance amount paid by the Plaintiff.

DAMAGES

15. The Plaintiff is entitled to the refund of the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- retained by the Defendant without consideration due to the abandonment of the project.

16. The Plaintiff has suffered consequential losses amounting to Rs. 12,00,000/- due to the delay in operations caused by the non-delivery of the ERP system. The assessment of such loss is supported by the loss assessment report prepared by the auditor, annexed hereto as **Annexure D**. The exact calculation method of consequential damages is as per {{CALCULATION_METHOD}}.

17. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 18% per annum on the total claim amount from the date of breach until realisation.

LEGAL BASIS

18. The Defendant has committed a breach of the agreement by failing to perform the obligations undertaken therein, thereby entitling the Plaintiff to claim damages under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

19. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

20. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

21. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

22. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

23. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

24. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

25. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

26. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) towards damages as particularised in the Schedule of Damages annexed hereto;
    (b) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (c) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (d) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

(i) Actual Loss
     Amount claimed: Rs. {{ACTUAL_LOSS_AMOUNT}}/-
     Basis / methodology: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_BASIS}}
     Documentary proof: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_PROOF}}

(ii) Consequential loss (within the contemplation of the parties at the time of contract, per Section 73 second limb / Hadley v. Baxendale)
     Amou
